blocks.normalized_text: holy father we eome to you today different ages galing po sa luzon visayas at mindanao iba-iba po ang paniniwala sa politika sa religion pero lahat po ay filipino at lahat po ay ginagalang ang pangalan mo sinabi ninyo po sa propetang jeremiah sa jeremiah 29 11 for i know the plans i have for you plans to prosper you and not to harm you plans to give you hope and a future you love us so much that you made us in your nature and likeness giving us free will thus to receive the fruits of your plans plans to prosper us and not to harm us plans to give us hope and a future we must first seek your kingdom and righteousness you called us to be faithful lord god for us to be faithful both in small things and in big things
blocks.raw_text: Holy Father, we eome to You today, different ages, galing po sa Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao, iba-iba po ang paniniwala sa politika, sa religion, pero lahat po ay Filipino at lahat po ay ginagalang ang Pangalan Mo. Sinabi Ninyo po sa Propetang Jeremiah, sa Jeremiah 29:11, “For I know the plans I have for you, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future. You love us so much that You made us in Your nature and likeness, giving us free will. Thus, to receive the fruits of Your plans—plans to prosper us and not to harm us, plans to give us hope and a future—we must first seek Your kingdom and righteousness. You called us to be faithful. Lord God, for us to be faithful both in small things and in big things.”
blocks.source_text:
Holy Father, we eome to You today, different ages, galing po sa Luzon, Visayas, at 
Mindanao, iba-iba po ang paniniwala sa politika, sa religion, pero lahat po ay 
Filipino at lahat po ay ginagalang ang Pangalan Mo. Sinabi Ninyo po sa Propetang 
Jeremiah, sa Jeremiah 29:11, “For I know the plans I have for you, plans to prosper 
you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future. You love us so much that 
You made us in Your nature and likeness, giving us free will. Thus, to receive the fruits 
of Your plans—plans to prosper us and not to harm us, plans to give us hope and a 
future—we must first seek Your kingdom and righteousness. You called us to be faithful. 
Lord God, for us to be faithful both in small things and in big things.”

blocks.normalized_text: same with the rules help us to be faithful to the constitution the senate rules and the rules of impeachment help us to be faithful to the rules of fairness as the prosecution stated yesterday to hold everyone accountable from the barangay captain to the highest official of the land lord god holy spirit do not allow us to obstruct justice yet always give us the strength to obstruct injustice regardless of the cause and the consequences magalit na po kung sinong magagalit gamitin ninyo kami bilang instrumento ng hustisya
blocks.raw_text: Same with the rules. Help us to be faithful to the Constitution, the Senate Rules, and the Rules of Impeachment. Help us to be faithful to the rules of fairness. As the prosecution stated yesterday, to hold everyone accountable, from the barangay captain to the highest official of the land. blocks.source_text:
Same with the rules. Help us to be faithful to the Constitution, the Senate Rules, 
and the Rules of Impeachment. Help us to be faithful to the rules of fairness. As the 
prosecution stated yesterday, to hold everyone accountable, from the barangay captain to 
the highest official of the land. Lord God, Holy Spirit, do not allow us to obstruct justice, 
yet always give us the strength to obstruct injustice, regardless of the cause and the 
consequences. Magalit na po kung sinong magagalit, gamitin Ninyo kami bilang 
instrumento ng hustisya.

blocks.raw_text: Lead each one of us to pursue the impeachment trial with fairness, political neutrality, and due process and to pursue the flood control scandal with the same zeal, effort, fairness, and political neutrality. To pursue corruption in all forms, help us build a justice system that is ideal, rather than one that is weaponized and is actually an instrument of oppression and tyranny.
blocks.source_text:
Lead each one of us to pursue the impeachment trial with fairness, political neutrality, 
and due process and to pursue the flood control scandal with the same zeal, effort, 
fairness, and political neutrality. To pursue corruption in all forms, help us build a justice 
system that is ideal, rather than one that is weaponized and is actually an instrument of 
oppression and tyranny.

the Philippine Constitution, which is found in the Preamble: “We, the sovereign Filipino

establish a government that shall embody our ideals and aspirations, promote the common

the blessings of independence and democracy, under the rule of law and a regime of truth, 
justice, freedom, love, equality, peace, do ordain and promulgate this Constitution.”

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge lacson sought clarification on whether the presiding officer s statement that conviction requires the affirmative vote of at least 16 senator-judges was a personal legal opinion or a ruling of the impeachment court on the premise that it was a ruling of the court he further inquired whether it has raised the level of discourse to a justiciable issue that only the supreme court has the legal authority to settle should it be challenged by either of the parties
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Lacson sought clarification on whether the Presiding Officer’s statement that conviction requires the affirmative vote of at least 16 Senator-Judges was a personal legal opinion or a ruling of the Impeachment Court. On the premise that it was a ruling of the Court, he further inquired whether, it has raised the level of discourse to a justiciable issue that only the Supreme Court has the legal authority to settle, should it be challenged by either of the parties.
blocks.section_title: POINT OF INQUIRY OF SENATOR-JUDGE LACSON
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Lacson sought clarification on whether the Presiding Officer’s statement that 
conviction requires the affirmative vote of at least 16 Senator-Judges was a personal legal opinion 
or a ruling of the Impeachment Court. On the premise that it was a ruling of the Court, he further 
inquired whether, it has raised the level of discourse to a justiciable issue that only the Supreme 
Court has the legal authority to settle, should it be challenged by either of the parties.

blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer confirmed that the statement was a ruling of the chair he noted that it had been clarified in response to an earlier inquiry by senator-judge cayetano a and recorded in the approved journal as no objecton or appeal had been made the ruling remained in effect he added that whether the matter constituted a justiciable controversy was for the courts to detennine
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer confirmed that the statement was a ruling of the Chair. He noted that it had been clarified in response to an earlier inquiry by Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) and recorded in the approved Journal. As no objecton or appeal had been made, the ruling remained in effect. He added that whether the matter constituted a justiciable controversy was for the courts to detennine.
blocks.section_title: POINT OF INQUIRY OF SENATOR-JUDGE LACSON
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer confirmed that the statement was a ruling of the Chair. He noted that 
it had been clarified in response to an earlier inquiry by Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) and recorded 
in the approved Journal. As no objecton or appeal had been made, the ruling remained in effect. 
He added that whether the matter constituted a justiciable controversy was for the courts to 
detennine.

blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) affirmed that he raised the question during the previous day’s 
proceedings. He recalled that, upon receiving confirmation that the Presiding Officer’s statement 
constituted a ruling of the Impeachment Court, he did not pursue the threshold issue.
blocks.speaker_raw: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) affirmed that he raised the question during the previous day’s proceedings

blocks.normalized_text: referring then to rule xxii of the rules of procedure on impeachment trials senator-judge cayetano a emphasized that orders and decisions of the court may be acted upon without objection or if an objection is raised shall be decided by a vote of the members without debate subject to the provisions of rule vi on questions related to evidence he likewise asserted that although he had raised an objection during the previous day s proceedings he had not been afforded the period prescribed to explain his objections nevertheless he acknowledged that during the discussion on the point of order senate president gatchalian has given everyone considerable latitude in explaining their respective positions and that he was instead allowed to submit his explanation in writing
blocks.raw_text: Referring then to Rule XXII of the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials, Senator- Judge Cayetano (A) emphasized that orders and decisions of the Court may be acted upon without objection, or, if an objection is raised, shall be decided by a vote of the Members without debate, subject to the provisions of Rule VI on questions related to evidence. He likewise asserted that, although he had raised an objection during the previous day’s proceedings, he had not been afforded the period prescribed to explain his objections. Nevertheless, he acknowledged that, during the discussion on the point of order. Senate President Gatchalian has given everyone considerable latitude in explaining their respective positions and that he was instead allowed to submit his explanation in writing.
blocks.source_text:
Referring then to Rule XXII of the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials, Senator- 
Judge Cayetano (A) emphasized that orders and decisions of the Court may be acted upon without 
objection, or, if an objection is raised, shall be decided by a vote of the Members without debate, 
subject to the provisions of Rule VI on questions related to evidence. He likewise asserted that, 
although he had raised an objection during the previous day’s proceedings, he had not been afforded 
the period prescribed to explain his objections. Nevertheless, he acknowledged that, during the 
discussion on the point of order. Senate President Gatchalian has given everyone considerable 
latitude in explaining their respective positions and that he was instead allowed to submit his 
explanation in writing.

blocks.normalized_text: aquino case before the supreme court likewise involved the interpretation of the phrase all members of the senate he maintained that under the high court s ruling all incumbent senators remained members of the senate unless they died resigned or were removed from office with finality and should therefore be counted in determining the two-thirds vote required for conviction he added that he intended to prepare a memorandum for the guidance of his fellow senator-judges particularly concerning the voting participation of senator-judges marcoleta and estrada he acknowledged that as of that day the impeachment court had ruled that conviction on any articles of impeachment required the affirmative votes of at least 16 of the senate s 24 members
blocks.raw_text: Aquino case before the Supreme Court likewise involved the interpretation of the phrase “all Members of the Senate.” He maintained that, under the High Court’s ruling, all incumbent senators remained Members of the Senate unless they died, resigned, or were removed from office with finality, and should therefore be counted in determining the two-thirds vote required for conviction. He added that he intended to prepare a memorandum for the guidance of his fellow Senator-Judges, particularly concerning the voting participation of Senator-Judges Marcoleta and Estrada. He acknowledged that, as of that day, the Impeachment Court had ruled that conviction on any Articles of Impeachment required the affirmative votes of at least 16 of the Senate’s 24 members.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) mentioned that the pending Cayetano v. Aquino case before the 
Supreme Court likewise involved the interpretation of the phrase “all Members of the Senate.” He 
maintained that, under the High Court’s ruling, all incumbent senators remained Members of the 
Senate unless they died, resigned, or were removed from office with finality, and should therefore 
be counted in determining the two-thirds vote required for conviction. He added that he intended 
to prepare a memorandum for the guidance of his fellow Senator-Judges, particularly concerning 
the voting participation of Senator-Judges Marcoleta and Estrada. He acknowledged that, as of that 
day, the Impeachment Court had ruled that conviction on any Articles of Impeachment required the 
affirmative votes of at least 16 of the Senate’s 24 members.

Procedure on Impeachment Trials to personally attend the proceedings, as she was already 
represented by counsel. He stated that the Court would act on any motion to require the 
respondent’s appearance once it is formally filed, after giving the defense an opportunity to 
comment.

blocks.normalized_text: request oe senator cayetano a
blocks.raw_text: REQUEST OE SENATOR CAYETANO (A)
blocks.source_text: REQUEST OE SENATOR CAYETANO (A)

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a requested time for the impeachment court to carefully study the legal issue of compelling witnesses to testify he explained that while the rules of court generally prohibits forcing an accused person to take the witness stand non-accused witnesses may be required to testify but can invoke the right against self-incrimination only in response to specific questions he then proposed that if the prosecution intended to seek a subpoena both the prosecution and the defense should submit memoranda in advance so the senator-judges can study the issue thoroughly before debating and ruling on it
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) requested time for the Impeachment Court to carefully study the legal issue of compelling witnesses to testify. He explained that while the Rules of Court generally prohibits forcing an accused person to take the witness stand, non-accused witnesses may be required to testify but can invoke the right against self-incrimination only in response to specific questions. He then proposed that if the prosecution intended to seek a subpoena, both the prosecution and the defense should submit memoranda in advance so the senator-judges can study the issue thoroughly before debating and ruling on it.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) requested time for the Impeachment Court to carefully study the 
legal issue of compelling witnesses to testify. He explained that while the Rules of Court generally 
prohibits forcing an accused person to take the witness stand, non-accused witnesses may be 
required to testify but can invoke the right against self-incrimination only in response to specific 
questions. He then proposed that if the prosecution intended to seek a subpoena, both the 
prosecution and the defense should submit memoranda in advance so the senator-judges can study 
the issue thoroughly before debating and ruling on it.

blocks.section_title: INTRODUCTION OF ARTICLE TV OE THE IMPEACHMENT CASE
blocks.source_text:
As Prosecutor Defensor began with a narrative describing the events underlying the charge. 
Defense Attorney Carlo Joaquin Narvasa objected, arguing that such a narration was not proper 
court procedure and that the prosecution should instead proceed directly to presenting its first 
witness. Prosecutor Defensor responded that the prosecution should be allowed to advocate its 
position through a brief introductory statement, noting that impeachment is a sui generis proceeding 
and that the introduction would help both the senator-judges and the public understand the evidence 
to be presented.

Honorable Senator-Judges, noong ika-22 ng Hitnyo, 2026, nagulat at nagdalamhati ang 
buong bansa sa pamamaril sa isang paaralan sa Tacloban City na ikinasawi ng tatlong batang 
mag-aaral at ikinasugat ng marami pang iba. Ang mga salarin, mga menor de edad. Sa kabila 
ng kanilang murang edad, agad na kumilos ang mga awtoridad upang ipatupad ang mga 
umiiral na batas. Ang mensahe ay malinaw: ang pagiging bata ay Hindi nangangahulugang 
walang pananagutan. Ang pananagutan ay umiiral bagama’t iba ang paraan ng pagpapatupad 
nito sa Halim ng batas.

The evidence presented in this Article are not just derived from testimonies and authenticated 
documents, but from the Vice President’s own words, own conduct, her very own public 
admissions. Her words were neither accidental nor taken out of context—they were uttered publicly, 
with the intention to be taken seriously. “Huwag kang mag-alala. Mam, sa security ko kasi may 
kinausap na ako na tao, at sinabi ko sa kanya na kapag pinatay ako, patayin mo si BBM, si 
Liza Marcos, si Martin Romualdez. No joke. No joke.”

blocks.raw_text: What makes these threats especially sinister is that they do not come from an ordinary citizen, but from the Vice-President herself, an official who, under the Constitution, stands to succeed the President in the Republic. Such statements carry a gravity far from the exercise of one’s freedom of expression; they strike at the very heart of government. This is especially significant given that she has publicly declared her intention to run for President in the next election.

What makes these threats especially sinister is that they do not come from an ordinary citizen, 
but from the Vice-President herself, an official who, under the Constitution, stands to succeed the 
President in the Republic. Such statements carry a gravity far from the exercise of one’s freedom 
of expression; they strike at the very heart of government. This is especially significant given that 
she has publicly declared her intention to run for President in the next election.

blocks.raw_text: The motion to exclude by the Counsel for Respondent is hereby denied. The Chair cites Enrile vs. Sandiganbayan, which states that the prosecutors may add or rely on evidence during pre-trial, even if the evidence is not stated in the complaint or articles, because only a summary is required to be stated in the Articles of Impeachment, in this case, and/or complaint, and not a complete list of the prosecution’s evidence and/or witnesses.
blocks.source_text:
The motion to exclude by the Counsel for Respondent is hereby denied. The Chair cites Enrile 
vs. Sandiganbayan, which states that the prosecutors may add or rely on evidence during pre-trial, 
even if the evidence is not stated in the complaint or articles, because only a summary is required 
to be stated in the Articles of Impeachment, in this case, and/or complaint, and not a complete list 
of the prosecution’s evidence and/or witnesses.

blocks.normalized_text: your honor please we do not want to put the defense on the spot but that is a misreading of the rules your honor please counsel cited rule 132 section 17 with all respect to the defense that doesn t serve your interest it serves the interest of the prosecution may i explain reason if we read section 17 of rule 132 it says when part of transaction writing a record given in evidence the remainder is admissible when parts of an act declaration conversation writing a record when part is given by one party and in this case that is the prosecution the whole of the same may be inquired into by the other side meaning it s simple this rule section 17 rule 132 allows exactly the prosecution to present a two-minute portion of a two-hour video subject to the defense the presentation of the remainder of the same be that as it may your honor please the rules are clear no 2 we invoke your honor please the ruling that it is for the prosecution to decide what to present when to present how to present and whether to present at all
blocks.raw_text: Your Honor, please, we do not want to put the Defense on the spot, but that is a misreading of the Rules. Your Honor, please, counsel cited Rule 132, Section 17. With all respect to the Defense, that doesn’t serve your interest; it serves the interest of the Prosecution. May I explain? Reason: If we read Section 17 of Rule 132, it says, “When part of transaction, writing a record given in evidence, the remainder is admissible. When parts of an act, declaration, conversation, writing a record, when part is given by one party,”—and in this case, that is the Prosecution—“the whole of the same may be inquired into by the other side.” Meaning, it’s simple: This Rule, Section 17, Rule 132, allows exactly the Prosecution to present a two-minute portion of a two-hour video subject to the Defense the presentation of the remainder of the same. Be that as it may. Your Honor, please, the Rules are clear; No. 2, we invoke. Your Honor, please, the ruling that it is for the Prosecution to decide what to present, when to present, how to present, and whether to present at all.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Ligutan. Your Honor, please, we do not want to put the Defense on the spot, but that 
is a misreading of the Rules. Your Honor, please, counsel cited Rule 132, Section 17. With all 
respect to the Defense, that doesn’t serve your interest; it serves the interest of the Prosecution. 
May I explain? Reason: If we read Section 17 of Rule 132, it says, “When part of transaction, 
writing a record given in evidence, the remainder is admissible. When parts of an act, declaration, 
conversation, writing a record, when part is given by one party,”—and in this case, that is the 
Prosecution—“the whole of the same may be inquired into by the other side.” Meaning, it’s simple: 
This Rule, Section 17, Rule 132, allows exactly the Prosecution to present a two-minute portion of 
a two-hour video subject to the Defense the presentation of the remainder of the same. Be that 
as it may. Your Honor, please, the Rules are clear; No. 2, we invoke. Your Honor, please, the ruling 
that it is for the Prosecution to decide what to present, when to present, how to present, and 
whether to present at all.

blocks.normalized_text: be that as it may your honor please the defense will not be prejudiced exactly because when the time comes for the defense to present their evidence they can present the remainder of that two-hour video what we are saying your honor please for the interest of time for the prosecution s presentation of evidence we deem that only a two-minute portion of that two-hour video is relevant at this point in time your honor please we move for a ruling
blocks.raw_text: Be that as it may. Your Honor, please, the Defense will not be prejudiced exactly, because when the time comes for the Defense to present their evidence, they can present the remainder of that two-hour video. What we are saying. Your Honor, please, for the interest of time, for the Prosecution's presentation of evidence, we deem that only a two-minute portion of that two-hour video is relevant at this point in time. blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer informed the court that during the recess he had conferred with the senator-judges including senator-judge cayetano a regarding the presentation of the video evidence he ruled that should the respondent elect to present the entire video as previously manifested by defense counsel the court would allow its presentation during the respondent s presentation of evidence he emphasized that the court would not interfere with either party s manner of presenting its case explaining that the prosecution s presentation of selected portions of the video was intended to support the allegations in the articles of impeachment and to promote the efficient conduct of the proceedings he likewise noted that the respondent would be afforded the same opportunity to present such evidence as it deemed necessary to establish its defense
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer informed the Court that, during the recess, he had conferred with the Senator-Judges, including Senator-Judge Cayetano (A), regarding the presentation of the video evidence. He ruled that, should the respondent elect to present the entire video, as previously manifested by defense counsel, the Court would allow its presentation during the respondent’s presentation of evidence. He emphasized that the Court would not interfere with either party’s manner of presenting its case, explaining that the prosecution’s presentation of selected portions of the video was intended to support the allegations in the Articles of Impeachment and to promote the efficient conduct of the proceedings. He likewise noted that the respondent would be afforded the same opportunity to present such evidence as it deemed necessary to establish its defense.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer informed the Court that, during the recess, he had conferred with the 
Senator-Judges, including Senator-Judge Cayetano (A), regarding the presentation of the video 
evidence. He ruled that, should the respondent elect to present the entire video, as previously 
manifested by defense counsel, the Court would allow its presentation during the respondent’s 
presentation of evidence. He emphasized that the Court would not interfere with either party’s 
manner of presenting its case, explaining that the prosecution’s presentation of selected portions of 
the video was intended to support the allegations in the Articles of Impeachment and to promote 
the efficient conduct of the proceedings. He likewise noted that the respondent would be afforded 
the same opportunity to present such evidence as it deemed necessary to establish its defense.

blocks.normalized_text: in as much as we understand that this statement may not actually 100 prove that she in fact contracted an assassin to kill the president your honor please we are invoking a rule in the rules of evidence that there could be statements utterances of a person that may not be directly similar to what he or she did but it could establish your honor please what we call a specific intent knowledge identity plan system and the like ibig sabihin po pimpakita ng prosecution na iyong mga nasabi ng vice-president sa gabing iyon november 23 2024 hindi po iyan nag-iisang statement ng vice-president kung hindi iyon po iyong culmination sa mga series of statements ng vice-president kung saan sinasabi niya tala gang gusto niyang patayin iyong presidente iyong first lady at saka iyong former speaker of the house your honor please
blocks.raw_text: In as much as we understand that this statement may not actually 100% prove that she in fact contracted an assassin to kill the President, Your Honor, please, we are invoking a rule in the Rules of Evidence that there could be statements, utterances of a person that may not be directly similar to what he or she did but it could establish. Your Honor, please, what we call a specific intent, knowledge, identity, plan, system, and the like. Ibig sabihin po, pimpakita ng prosecution na iyong mga nasabi ng Vice-President sa gabing iyon, November 23, 2024, hindi po iyan nag-iisang statement ng Vice-President. Kung hindi, iyon po iyong culmination sa mga series of statements ng Vice-President kung saan sinasabi niya tala gang gusto niyang patayin iyong Presidente, iyong First Lady, at saka iyong former Speaker of the House, Your Honor, please.
blocks.source_text:
In as much as we understand that this statement may not actually 100% prove that she in fact 
contracted an assassin to kill the President, Your Honor, please, we are invoking a rule in the Rules 
of Evidence that there could be statements, utterances of a person that may not be directly similar 
to what he or she did but it could establish. Your Honor, please, what we call a specific intent, 
knowledge, identity, plan, system, and the like. Ibig sabihin po, pimpakita ng prosecution na 
iyong mga nasabi ng Vice-President sa gabing iyon, November 23, 2024, hindi po iyan nag-iisang 
statement ng Vice-President. Kung hindi, iyon po iyong culmination sa mga series of statements 
ng Vice-President kung saan sinasabi niya tala gang gusto niyang patayin iyong Presidente, 
iyong First Lady, at saka iyong former Speaker of the House, Your Honor, please.

blocks.normalized_text: we invoke rule 130 your honor please section 35 of the rules of court itong mga statements na ito they prove one thing talagang may piano na talagang may system na talagang may knowledge na iyong vice-president sa anong gusto niyang gawin sa pangulo gusto niyang pugutan ng ulo gusto niyang patayin no less than the president of the republic of the philippines your honor please
blocks.raw_text: We invoke Rule 130, Your Honor, please, Section 35 of the Rules of Court. Itong mga statements na ito, they prove one thing: talagang may piano na, talagang may system na, talagang may knowledge na iyong Vice-President sa anong gusto niyang gawin sa Pangulo: gusto niyang pugutan ng ulo; gusto niyang patayin. No less than the President of the Republic of the Philippines, Your Honor, please.
blocks.source_text:
We invoke Rule 130, Your Honor, please, Section 35 of the Rules of Court. Itong mga

blocks.normalized_text: to reply senator-judge erwin t tulfo the court did not adopt the judicial affidavit rule for the simple reason that we wanted all of the testimonies to be made and given in open court so that it will be heard not only by the senator-judges but also by the public as a whole given that this is an impeachment court and an impeachment trial that seeks to unseat a duly elected official
blocks.raw_text: To reply, Senator-Judge Erwin T. Tulfo, the Court did not adopt the Judicial Affidavit Rule for the simple reason that we wanted all of the testimonies to be made and given in open court, so that it will be heard not only by the senator-judges but also by the public as a whole, given that this is an Impeachment Court and an impeachment trial that seeks to unseat a duly elected official.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer. To reply, Senator-Judge Erwin T. Tulfo, the Court did not adopt the 
Judicial Affidavit Rule for the simple reason that we wanted all of the testimonies to be made and 
given in open court, so that it will be heard not only by the senator-judges but also by the public 
as a whole, given that this is an Impeachment Court and an impeachment trial that seeks to unseat 
a duly elected official.

At this juncture, the following is the text of the video presentation, with Vice President Sara 
Zimmerman Duterte speaking:
“Me, another person warned me, and then I was asked, during a press briefing, by 
a media person if I know of these plans, if you can say the word—if that is the correct 
word to use here—plans. So, I answered, I was asked, Romanov, and then I answered—
1 was told by two people—this is the first time I have heard of Romanov. So, that is the 
third already, and I said, ‘Don’t worry about my security, if they kill me, if I die, kapag 
pinatay ako o kapag namatay ako, in the context of 'don't wony about my security. ’
